creator: mat@FreeBSD.org
text:
Hi,

+--On 9 janvier 2014 16:23:53 +0100 Fabian Wenk <fabian@wenks.ch> wrote:
| I have just updated my Ports [1] and got the update from dns/bind99
| (bind99-9.9.4_2 -> bind99-9.9.4_9) on FreeBSD 9.1-RELEASE-p9.
| A few days ago with the update of an other port (in my case jabberd)
| openssl from the Ports was pulled in. Before I had only system openssl
| installed. After the upgrade with restart of bind99 with '/etc/rc.d/named
| restart' I got the same errors as you already reported yourself in this
| PR.
| 
| I did the following to fix this:
| mkdir -p /var/named/usr/local/lib/engines/
| cp -p /usr/local/lib/engines/* /var/named/usr/local/lib/engines/
|
| As I see it, the fix in r338882 [2] did not touch / change anything
| related to lib/engines. I think somehow updating lib/engines in the bind
| / named chroot should be done from the openssl port, but sure it needs a
| restart of named to take effect. Eventually this is something which
| should go into the /etc/rc.d/named script to also update / create the
| lib/engines in the named chroot if openssl is installed from Ports.

Ah, I had forgotten all about that after I fixed the port not to remove
everything in /var/named/usr/local.
It does need to go in the named rc script, but I can't really touch the one
in /etc/rc.d/ that is used before 10, and the one I install after 10
doesn't have chroot any more.

I'll need to have to think about it real hard :-)

-- 
Mathieu Arnold
